message:
refactor(discord,telegram): extract platform limit constants into limits.rs modules

Centralizes Discord and Telegram API limit constants (message length,
embed counts, caption length, thread name length) into dedicated
limits.rs files so validation code and boundary tests reference named
constants instead of duplicated magic numbers. Fixes previously
tautological limit-constant tests to actually validate the values.

Discord: MESSAGE_CONTENT_MAX_CHARS (2000), EMBEDS_MAX_COUNT (10),
EMBED_TOTAL_MAX_CHARS (6000), EMBED_TITLE_MAX_CHARS (256),
EMBED_DESCRIPTION_MAX_CHARS (4096), THREAD_NAME_MAX_CHARS (100).

Telegram: MESSAGE_TEXT_MAX_CHARS (4096), MEDIA_CAPTION_MAX_CHARS (1024).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+//! Telegram platform limits used by validation and boundary tests.
+//!
+//! TODO: Add callback-data, media-group, and upload-size limits when those
+//! operations are implemented.
+
+/// Maximum characters allowed in message text.
+pub const MESSAGE_TEXT_MAX_CHARS: usize = 4_096;
+/// Maximum characters allowed in media captions.
+pub const MEDIA_CAPTION_MAX_CHARS: usize = 1_024;
